description: The world's best-preserved ancient castle-style city gate, an important part of the Nanjing City Wall.
A double-helix scenic pedestrian bridge over the Yangtze River, a modern architectural landmark offering views of the river and city skyline.
The residence of Chiang Kai-shek and Soong Mei-ling, featuring French architectural style, known as the 'First Villa of the Far East.'
A Qinhuai River water sightseeing experience, showcasing ancient architecture and night views along the banks, capturing the charm of the Six Dynasties ancient capital.
The site where the Treaty of Nanking was signed, an important witness to modern history.
A commercial district with a Republican-era ambiance, featuring dining, entertainment, and shopping, with vibrant night views popular among young people.
A Ming Dynasty pavilion-style building, a commanding viewpoint along the Yangtze River with expansive views.

attraction_name: Purple Mountain Observatory of the Chinese Academy of Sciences
description: The birthplace of modern Chinese astronomy, offering panoramic views of Nanjing and serving as a science education base.
The ruins of a Southern Dynasty ancient temple, now a cemetery for fallen soldiers of the National Revolutionary Army, with the Linggu Pagoda as its iconic structure.
A famous food street in Nanjing, featuring a variety of local snacks and night market delicacies, offering an authentic taste of Nanjing.
A sacred site commemorating the Chinese New Democratic Revolution, a solemn place to honor revolutionary martyrs.
China's first national institution of higher learning, located in the core scenic area of the Qinhuai River, with well-preserved ancient architectural complexes.
A famous ancient temple in Nanjing, now a cemetery for revolutionary martyrs, with a serene environment and the Linggu Pagoda as its symbol.
The mausoleum of Dr. Sun Yat-sen, the founding father of the Republic of China, a core attraction in the Zhongshan Scenic Area.

attraction_name: Music Stage at Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum Scenic Area in Zhongshan Scenic Area
description: An open-air music stage built during the Republic of China era, featuring exquisite architecture and a tranquil surrounding environment.
One of China's three major museums, with extensive collections including six exhibition halls: History Hall, Art Hall, Republic Hall, and more.
